devanagari: इक्ष्वाकूणां कुशादिसुमित्रान्तानां वर्णनम् । श्रीशुक उवाच । कुशस्य चातिथिस्तस्मात् निषधस्तत्सुतो नभः । पुण्डरीकोऽथ तत्पुत्रः क्षेमधन्वाभ...
english_devnagari: ikṣvākūṇāṃ kuśādisumitrāntānāṃ varṇanamśrīśuka uvācakuśasya cātithistasmāt niṣadhastatsuto nabhaḥpuṇḍarīko'tha tatputraḥ kṣemadhanvābhavattataḥ
translation:
Śrī Śuka said:
From Kuśa’s (Rāma’s son’s) loins sprang up Atithi who gave birth to Niṣadha, whose son was Nabha. Nabha’s son was Puṇḍarīka from whom was born Kṣemadhanvā.

devanagari: देवानीकस्ततोऽनीहः पारियात्रोऽथ तत्सुतः । ततो बलस्थलः तस्मात् वज्रनाभोऽर्कसंभवः ॥ २ ॥
english_devnagari: devānīkastato'nīhaḥ pāriyātro'tha tatsutaḥtato balasthalaḥ tasmāt vajranābho'rkasaṃbhavaḥ
translation: His son was Devānīka who had a son called Anīha, whose son was Pāriyātra. His son was Bala from whom was born Sthala[1] (Cala), and his son Vajranābha was born from an aṃśa—ray—of the Sun-god.
purport:
Footnote [1]:
Anvitārthaprakāśikā. Bālaprabodhini Bhaktamanorañjanī take Bala & Sthala as father and son. B.P. specifically states:—balasya sthala-nāmā putro'bhavad ityarthaḥ.

devanagari: खसगणः तत्सुतः तस्माद् विधृतिश्चाभवत्सुतः । ततो हिरण्यनाभोऽभूद् योगाचार्यस्तु जैमिनेः ॥ ३ ॥
english_devnagari: khasagaṇaḥ tatsutaḥ tasmād vidhṛtiścābhavatsutaḥtato hiraṇyanābho'bhūd yogācāryastu jaimineḥ
translation: His son was Khagaṇa from whom Vidhṛti was born as a son. From Vidhṛti sprang Hiraṇyanābha who became a preceptor of yoga and a pupil of Jaimini.[2]
purport:
Footnote [2]:
Can this be the author of the Sūtras of Pūrvamīmāṃsā or the pupil of Vyāsa as Hiraṇyanābha’s 14th descendant Bṛhaḍbala was a contemporary of Pāṇḍavas? He is probably some ancient sage of the same name.

devanagari: शिष्यः कौशल्य आध्यात्मं याज्ञवल्क्योऽध्यगाद् यतः । योगं महोदयं ऋषिः हृदयग्रन्थि भेदकम् ॥ ४ ॥
english_devnagari: śiṣyaḥ kauśalya ādhyātmaṃ yājñavalkyo'dhyagād yataḥyogaṃ mahodayaṃ ṛṣiḥ hṛdayagranthi bhedakam
translation: It was from Hiraṇyanābha that the sage Yājñavalkya of Kosala got instructions in yoga pertaining to the science of the soul (spiritual lore), which leads to the attainment of great mystical powers (ṣiddhis), and cuts asunder the knot of ego or ignorance in the heart.

devanagari: पुष्यो हिरण्यनाभस्य ध्रुवसन्धिः ततोऽभवत् । सुदर्शनोऽथाग्निवर्णः शीघ्रस्तस्य मरुः सुतः ॥ ५ ॥
english_devnagari: puṣyo hiraṇyanābhasya dhruvasandhiḥ tato'bhavatsudarśano'thāgnivarṇaḥ śīghrastasya maruḥ sutaḥ
translation: Hiraṇyanābha’s son was Puṣya from whom was born Dhruvasandhi. His son was Agnivarṇa, the father of Śīghra who had Maru as his son.

devanagari: सोऽसावास्ते योगसिद्धः कलापग्राममास्थितः । कलेरन्ते सूर्यवंशं नष्टं भावयिता पुनः ॥ ६ ॥
english_devnagari: so'sāvāste yogasiddhaḥ kalāpagrāmamāsthitaḥkalerante sūryavaṃśaṃ naṣṭaṃ bhāvayitā punaḥ
translation: It is this Maru who, having perfect mastery over yoga, is still residing at a village called Kalāpa[3] and at the end of Kali Age, he will revive the extinct solar race again.
purport:
Footnote [3]:
Kalāpa-grāma: A village situated near Badarikāśrama vide Bhāgavata Purāṇa. 10.87.7. From Nārada P. II. 67.6 it appears that Nārada and other sages were residents of this place. Here Maru and Devāpi, the last kings of the Solar and Lunar races, perform penance and wait to re-establish their dynasties at Ayodhyā and Hastināpura after Kalki, the tenth incarnation of Viṣṇu, has established social and religious order. According to the Vāyu P. 91.7, Kalāpa is near Meru where Purūravas and Ūrvaśī honeymooned. N.L. De on the authority of Col. Raper locates it near the source of Sarasvatī—a tributary of the Alakanandā, in Badrīnath in Garhwal—GDAMI P 84.

devanagari: तस्मात् प्रसुश्रुतः तस्य सन्धिः तस्याप्यमर्षणः । महस्वांन् तत्सुतः तस्माद् विश्वबाहुरजायत ॥ ७ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmāt prasuśrutaḥ tasya sandhiḥ tasyāpyamarṣaṇaḥmahasvāṃn tatsutaḥ tasmād viśvabāhurajāyata
translation: Maru’s son was Pra-Suśruta (or Suśruta), whose son was Sandhi, his son Amarṣaṇa, who gave birth to Mahasvān, from whom was born Viśvasāhva.

devanagari: ततः प्रसेनजित् तस्मात् तक्षको भविता पुनः । ततो बृहद्‍बलो यस्तु पित्रा ते समरे हतः ॥ ८ ॥
english_devnagari: tataḥ prasenajit tasmāt takṣako bhavitā punaḥtato bṛhad‍balo yastu pitrā te samare hataḥ
translation: Viśvasāhva’s son was Prasenajit whose son was Takṣaka; his son was Bṛhadbala who was killed in the battle by your father (Abhimanyu).

devanagari: एते हि ईक्ष्वाकुभूपाला अतीताः श्रृण्वनागतान् । बृहद्‍बलस्य भविता पुत्रो नाम्ना बृहद्रणः ॥ ९ ॥
english_devnagari: ete hi īkṣvākubhūpālā atītāḥ śrṛṇvanāgatānbṛhad‍balasya bhavitā putro nāmnā bṛhadraṇaḥ
translation: These are the kings of the Ikṣvāku race in the past. Now listen to the names of kings who are yet to come in future. Bṛhadbala will have (?) a son by name Bṛhadraṇa[4].
purport:
Footnote [4]:
If Bṛhadbala is killed by Abhimanyu, Bṛhadraṇa must have been born after the Bhārata war at the most, while Śuka is narrating these two generations after the Bhārata war—The Translator.

devanagari: ऊरुक्रियः सुतस्तस्य वत्सवृद्धो भविष्यति । प्रतिव्योमस्ततो भानुः दिवाको वाहिनीपतिः ॥ १० ॥
english_devnagari: ūrukriyaḥ sutastasya vatsavṛddho bhaviṣyatiprativyomastato bhānuḥ divāko vāhinīpatiḥ
translation: Bṛhadraṇa’s son will be Urukriya, whose son will be Vatsavṛddha; from him will spring Prativyoma whose son will be Bhānu; Bhānu’s son will be Divāka the leader of the celestial army.

devanagari: सहदेवस्ततो वीरो बृहदश्वोऽथ भानुमान् । प्रतीकाश्वो भानुमतः सुप्रतीकोऽथ तत्सुतः ॥ ११ ॥
english_devnagari: sahadevastato vīro bṛhadaśvo'tha bhānumānpratīkāśvo bhānumataḥ supratīko'tha tatsutaḥ
translation: Divāka’s son will be the great hero Sahadeva whose son will be Bṛhadaśva of whom will be born Bhānumān. Bhānumān’s son will be Pratīkāśva whose son will be Supratīka.

devanagari: भविता मरुदेवोऽथ सुनक्षत्रोऽथ पुष्करः । तस्यान्तरिक्षः तत्पुत्रः सुतपास्तद् अमित्रजित् ॥ १२ ॥
english_devnagari: bhavitā marudevo'tha sunakṣatro'tha puṣkaraḥtasyāntarikṣaḥ tatputraḥ sutapāstad amitrajit
translation: From him will be born Marudeva, whose son will be Sunakṣatra. Thence will be Puṣkara whose son will be Antarīkṣa; his son will be Sutapas, the father of Amitrajit.

devanagari: बृहद्राजस्तु तस्यापि बर्हिस्तस्मात् कृतञ्जयः । रणञ्जयस्तस्य सुतः सञ्जयो भविता ततः ॥ १३ ॥
english_devnagari: bṛhadrājastu tasyāpi barhistasmāt kṛtañjayaḥraṇañjayastasya sutaḥ sañjayo bhavitā tataḥ
translation: From him will be born Bṛhadraja, whose son will be Barhis; thence Kṛtañjaya, whose son will be Raṇañjaya; thence will be born Sañjaya.

devanagari: तस्माच्छाक्योऽथ शुद्धोदो लांगलस्तत्सुतः स्मृतः । ततः प्रसेनजित् तस्मात् क्षुद्रको भविता ततः ॥ १४ ॥
english_devnagari: tasmācchākyo'tha śuddhodo lāṃgalastatsutaḥ smṛtaḥtataḥ prasenajit tasmāt kṣudrako bhavitā tataḥ
translation: From him will be born Śākya; of him Śuddhoda from whom in turn will spring Lāṅgala; thence will be Prasenajit from whom will be born Kṣudraka.

devanagari: रणको भविता तस्मात् सुरथस्तनयस्ततः । सुमित्रो नाम निष्ठान्त एते बार्हद्‍बलान्वयाः ॥ १५ ॥
english_devnagari: raṇako bhavitā tasmāt surathastanayastataḥsumitro nāma niṣṭhānta ete bārhad‍balānvayāḥ
translation: From Kṣudraka will spring Raṇaka who will have a son called Suratha. Suratha’s son Sumitra will be the last scion—These will be the kings descended in the race of Bṛhadbala.

devanagari: इक्ष्वाकूणां अयं वंशः सुमित्रान्तो भविष्यति । यतस्तं प्राप्य राजानं संस्थां प्राप्स्यति वै कलौ ॥ १६ ॥
english_devnagari: ikṣvākūṇāṃ ayaṃ vaṃśaḥ sumitrānto bhaviṣyatiyatastaṃ prāpya rājānaṃ saṃsthāṃ prāpsyati vai kalau
translation:
The race of Ikṣvākus will end up with Sumitra for when he ascends the throne, the race of Ikṣvāku will be terminated and extinct in the Kali age.
[back to top]
